message:
fix(security): prevent log leakage in JSON checksum verification

Refactor verify_all_installers_json to capture stderr in a temporary file
instead of mixing it with stdout. This prevents informational logs (like
retry attempts) from corrupting the captured checksum hash, which was
causing false mismatches in JSON reports.

files.patch:
@@ -1067,9 +1067,28 @@ verify_all_installers_json() {
             continue
         fi
 
-        local actual
+        local actual=""
         local fetch_error=""
-        actual=$(fetch_checksum "$url" 2>&1) || fetch_error="$actual"
+        local tmp_err=""
+
+        # Create temp file for stderr capture
+        if command -v mktemp &>/dev/null; then
+            tmp_err=$(mktemp 2>/dev/null) || tmp_err="/tmp/acfs-err-$RANDOM"
+        else
+            tmp_err="/tmp/acfs-err-$RANDOM"
+        fi
+
+        # Capture stdout to variable, stderr to file
+        if actual=$(fetch_checksum "$url" 2>"$tmp_err"); then
+            # Success: actual contains the hash
+            :
+        else
+            # Failure: read error from stderr capture
+            fetch_error=$(cat "$tmp_err")
+            # Fallback if empty
+            [[ -z "$fetch_error" ]] && fetch_error="Unknown error fetching checksum"
+        fi
+        rm -f "$tmp_err"
 
         if [[ -n "$fetch_error" ]]; then
             local escaped_error
